ads.title: Brand introduction — Brandon Beck
primary_text:
Confluence Financial Partners invites you to an evening at J Alexander's in Naples.

Thursday, June 18 at 6:00 PM.

Brandon Beck — Wealth Manager — will lead a conversation designed to bring clarity to the financial decisions that matter most right now.

Topics for the evening:
→ Planning considerations for your future
→ Aligning family through regular, structured meetings
→ Protecting wealth with a clear estate strategy
→ Giving with intention to reflect your values and long-term impact

Complimentary dinner and cocktails. Couples and friends are welcome.

Are you thinking about the financial decisions that will shape the next chapter of your life?

Confluence Financial Partners is hosting an evening in Naples designed to explore the topics that matter most — from forward-looking planning to family alignment, estate strategy, and intentional giving.

Join Brandon Beck — Wealth Manager — for dinner at J Alexander's on Thursday, June 18 at 6:00 PM.

Complimentary dinner and cocktails. Couples and friends are welcome.

An evening in Naples designed for thoughtful conversation about your financial future.

Brandon Beck — Wealth Manager at Confluence Financial Partners — will be joined by a small group of guests for dinner at J Alexander's.

Thursday, June 18 at 6:00 PM.

The evening will focus on planning considerations for your future, family alignment, estate strategy, and giving with intention.

Complimentary dinner and cocktails. Couples and friends are welcome.
headline: An Evening of Clarity
A Thoughtful Evening in Naples
Conversations Worth Having
description: An Evening with Confluence Financial Partners

ads.title: Personal invitation — Brandon Beck
primary_text:
Brandon Beck — Wealth Manager at Confluence Financial Partners — works with individuals and families navigating the intersection of financial planning, estate coordination, and intentional wealth management.

He is hosting an intimate dinner in Naples to share perspectives on the decisions that can matter most at this stage of life.

Thursday, June 18 at 6:00 PM at J Alexander's.

An evening of thoughtful conversation, complimentary dinner, and cocktails. Couples and friends are welcome.

Meet Brandon Beck — Wealth Manager at Confluence Financial Partners.

Brandon works with families who are planning ahead, aligning around shared financial goals, and seeking clarity on how their decisions can work together.

He would like to invite you to an intimate dinner at J Alexander's in Naples.

Thursday, June 18 at 6:00 PM. Complimentary. Couples and friends are welcome.

We would value the opportunity to meet you.

When financial planning is approached thoughtfully, each decision can reinforce the ones around it. Your future planning, estate strategy, family conversations, and giving intentions are connected — and the right conversation can help bring that into focus.

Brandon Beck — Wealth Manager at Confluence Financial Partners — is hosting an evening in Naples to explore these connections.

Thursday, June 18 at J Alexander's. Complimentary dinner and cocktails.
headline: An Invitation from Brandon Beck

Join Brandon Beck in Naples

**Changes from Pittsburgh:** Brandon Beck replaces Greg Weimer (CEO). Consider warmer background treatment to match Naples venue tone.

ads.title: For Naples Families
primary_text:
For families who call Southwest Florida home, the financial considerations of this chapter deserve a thoughtful conversation — not a lecture.

Confluence Financial Partners is hosting an intimate dinner at J Alexander's in Naples for a small group of guests, designed to explore the topics that matter most: planning for your future, aligning family through structured meetings, protecting wealth with a clear estate strategy, and giving with intention.

Brandon Beck — Wealth Manager — will guide the evening.

Thursday, June 18 at 6:00 PM. Complimentary dinner and cocktails. A few seats remain.

This is not a seminar. It is a dinner.

Brandon Beck — Wealth Manager at Confluence Financial Partners — is hosting an intimate evening at J Alexander's in Naples for a small group of guests. The conversation will focus on forward-looking planning, family alignment, estate strategy, and intentional giving — considerations that may deserve more than a phone call.

Thursday, June 18. Complimentary dinner and cocktails. A few seats remain.

Confluence Financial Partners works with individuals and families who have built meaningful wealth and seek clarity about what comes next.

For those in Southwest Florida, an evening at J Alexander's in Naples offers the opportunity to explore how planning considerations, estate strategy, and intentional giving can work together in a setting designed for real conversation.

Thursday, June 18 at 6:00 PM. Brandon Beck hosting. Complimentary. A few seats remain.
headline: For Naples Families
A Dinner, Not a Seminar
For Southwest Florida Families
description: Thu, June 18 | A Few Seats Remain

ads.title: Naples setting + topics
primary_text:
Your estate plan, your giving strategy, and your family's financial alignment all influence each other — but they are often addressed in separate conversations.

Brandon Beck — Wealth Manager at Confluence Financial Partners — is hosting an intimate dinner in Naples to explore how these areas can work together.

Thursday, June 18 at 6:00 PM at J Alexander's.

The evening covers:
→ Planning considerations for your future
→ Aligning family through regular, structured meetings
→ Protecting wealth with a clear estate strategy
→ Giving with intention to reflect your values

Complimentary dinner and cocktails. Couples and friends are welcome.

What does your financial plan look like when all the pieces are considered together?

Join Brandon Beck — Wealth Manager — for an evening at J Alexander's in Naples designed to explore the connections between your forward-looking plan, family alignment, estate strategy, and the giving intentions that reflect your values.

Thursday, June 18 at 6:00 PM.

Complimentary dinner and cocktails. Couples and friends are welcome.

Whether you are thinking about planning for your future, aligning family through regular meetings, protecting wealth with a clear estate strategy, or giving with intention — each of these areas deserves thoughtful attention, and each one interacts with the others.

An evening at J Alexander's in Naples is designed to bring them together.

Brandon Beck — Wealth Manager at Confluence Financial Partners — will guide the conversation.

Thursday, June 18 at 6:00 PM. Complimentary dinner and cocktails. Couples and friends are welcome.
headline: What the Evening Covers
Relevant Topics, One Evening
When the Pieces Work Together

ads.title: Advisor perspective — Brandon Beck
primary_text:
Your estate plan, family financial conversations, and giving intentions each carry weight on their own. But the real clarity often comes from how they work together — especially as your circumstances evolve.

Is your estate strategy aligned with your family's current needs? Are you having the kinds of conversations that bring clarity to the generations that follow?

Join Brandon Beck for an evening in Naples exploring how coordination across these areas can help bring clarity and confidence to the decisions ahead.

Thursday, June 18 at J Alexander's. Complimentary dinner and cocktails. A few seats remain.

The decisions you make about your financial future, your estate plan, and your family's alignment can shape your legacy for decades. Each area deserves thoughtful attention — and they are more connected than they may appear.

Confluence Financial Partners is hosting an intimate dinner in Naples to explore how thoughtful coordination across these areas can help strengthen your overall plan.

Thursday, June 18 at 6:00 PM at J Alexander's. Complimentary. A few seats remain.

A well-coordinated financial plan considers more than any single piece in isolation. When your forward-looking strategy, estate plan, family alignment, and giving intentions work together, each decision can carry more purpose.

Brandon Beck — Wealth Manager at Confluence Financial Partners — is hosting a dinner in Naples focused on these connections.

Thursday, June 18 at J Alexander's. Complimentary dinner and cocktails. A few seats remain.
headline: Clarity and Confidence
Coordination That Matters
The Questions Worth Asking Now
ads.hook: Your estate plan, family conversations, and giving intentions — are they working together?

stories.image: Brandon Beck headshot (upper portion) with gradient to navy. Expertise-focused headline below.

**Photo direction:** Same photo-under structure as Pittsburgh Ad 7. Brandon Beck replaces CIO Bill Winkeler. Headline shifts from market/economy angle to holistic wealth management angle. Use same headshot as Ad 2, or a second photo if available for visual variety.

**Design note:** This runs in Wave 3 after the audience has seen Brandon in Ads 1 and 2. The headline should feel like expertise, not warmth — different register from Ad 2's "A Personal Invitation."

**Changes from Pittsburgh:** Brandon Beck replaces Bill Winkeler (CIO). Wealth management angle replaces market outlook. Same photo-under layout.

ads.title: Final seats — personal background
primary_text:
Thursday, June 18 is approaching, and a small number of seats remain at J Alexander's in Naples.

Join Brandon Beck — Wealth Manager at Confluence Financial Partners — for an evening focused on the financial considerations that matter most right now. Forward-looking planning, family alignment, estate strategy, and the kind of intentional giving that can help bring clarity to the years ahead.

Complimentary dinner and cocktails will be served.

We would value the opportunity to meet you.

An intimate dinner. A meaningful conversation. A few remaining seats.

Brandon Beck is hosting an evening at J Alexander's in Naples on Thursday, June 18 at 6:00 PM. The evening is designed for individuals and families who are planning ahead, aligning their families around shared goals, or seeking clarity about the path forward.

Complimentary dinner and cocktails. We would value the opportunity to meet you.

June 18 is approaching.

A small number of seats remain for an evening at J Alexander's in Naples with Brandon Beck — Wealth Manager at Confluence Financial Partners.

The evening is designed to bring together the considerations that matter most right now — planning ahead, aligning family, protecting wealth through a clear estate strategy, and giving with intention.

Complimentary dinner and cocktails. Couples and friends are welcome.

ads.image_direction:
**Format:** 1080x1080 (feed) + 1080x1920 (stories) | **Placement:** Feed, Stories
**Layout:** Photo-background with scarcity text overlay
**Pittsburgh reference:** Merges Ads 8 + 9. REPLACES text-on-navy approach with personal/photo background per Gladson's direction.

```
FEED (1080x1080):
+--------------------------------------+
|                                      |
|  [Background: Evening table          |
|   setting — warm amber lighting,     |
|   white tablecloth, wine glasses,    |
|   candles, dark wood. The kind of    |
|   table you'd want to sit at.        |
|   Dimmed to ~40% over navy base.]    |
|                                      |
|  LOGO (top-center, white)            |
|                                      |
|  "A Few Seats Left."                 |
|  (Ivypresto, white, 72px,           |

|                                      |

+--------------------------------------+

STORIES (1080x1920):
+--------------------+
| [TOP SAFE: 320px]  |
|                    |
| [Evening table     |
|  setting photo,    |
|  full-bleed,       |
|  dimmed ~40%]      |

| LOGO (white)       |
|                    |

|  Left."            |
| (Ivypresto, white, |

| Thursday, June 18 |
| J Alexander's      |
| (Rival Sans, 70%)  |
|                    |
| [Gold accent line]  |
|                    |
| [BTM SAFE: 400px]  |
| [CTA sticker,      |
|  gold border]      |
+--------------------+
```

**Photo direction:** NOT the text-on-navy approach from Pittsburgh Ads 8/9. Use a personal background similar to Ad 2's approach — an evening table setting that makes the viewer picture themselves at the dinner. Think: intimate private dining room, warm candlelight, place settings ready for guests, dark wood, amber tones. The photo should feel like the empty chair is waiting for them.

**J Alexander's interior reference:** Dark wood paneling, warm lighting, white tablecloths, leather booth seating. The photo should feel like J Alexander's itself — or at minimum, the same tier of upscale casual American dining.

**Changes from Pittsburgh:** Two ads → one. Text-on-navy → photo background. Same urgency copy. Gold accent retained for CTA.
